purpose: Identifies the sequential pattern of disinflation and associated risk windows, particularly the mission accomplished trap during the Phase 2 shelter-to-services transition
components: Phase 1: Goods disinflation via supply chain normalization and base effects
Phase 2: Shelter/OER deceleration delivering 40-80bp mechanical headline CPI relief
Phase 3: Services ex-shelter resolution — the binding constraint, historically requiring demand destruction
Trap identification: 4/6 base rate for premature 'mission accomplished' during Phase 2
current_assessment: Currently transitioning from Phase 1 to Phase 2. OER deceleration expected Q2-Q4 2026. Services ex-shelter at 3.5-4.0% with strong unit labor cost support. The trap window is the highest-conviction near-term risk across all agents.
limitations: Pattern-matched from only 6 episodes with researcher degrees of freedom in defining phases
Susceptible to hindsight bias without pre-registered predictions
Confidence 6/10 — useful heuristic, not a precision timing model
Cannot time factor rotations or sector trades at the specificity some agents attempt
